11   The line y = mx + 5, where m > 0, is normal to the curve y = 10 − x2 at the
     point (p, q).

     What is the value of p ?
         √
           2
     A    6
             √
     B − 62
          √
         3 2
     C    2
               √
     D −322
       √
     E   5
        √
     F − 5




                                           13

stem.rawText:
The line y = mx + 5, where m > 0, is normal to the curve y = 10 − x2 at the
     point (p, q).

     What is the value of p ?
         √
           2

solution.rawText:
We can find the equation of the normal to the curve y = 10 − x2 at the point (p, q) as usual.
                  dy
The derivative is    = −2x so at x = p the tangent has gradient −2p. Therefore the normal
                  dx
              1
has gradient    .
             2p
The tangent and normal both pass through the point (p, 10 − p2 ), which shows that q = 10 − p2 .
The normal thus has equation
                                                        1
                                  y − (10 − p2 ) =        (x − p)
                                                       2p
so rearranging into the form y = mx + c gives
                                          1    1
                                    y=      x − + 10 − p2
                                         2p    2
We want this to have the form y = mx + 5 where m > 0, so we have
                                       1
                                      − + 10 − p2 = 5.
                                       2
Rearranging thus gives
                                                   9
                                            p2 =
                                                   2
                       √
                3     3 2
and hence p = ± √ = ±
                 2     2
                                            √
           1                               3 2
Since m =    > 0 we must have p > 0 so p =
          2p                                2
The correct answer is C.
